Yet, it remains to be seen how the acquisition of Isaiah Joe could impact Detroit’s pursuit of Powell.

item.title: Landale, 30, is expected to have a strong market, …
item.description: Landale, 30, is expected to have a strong market, including interest from the Hawks, Chicago Bulls, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Philadelphia 76ers, and Cleveland Cavaliers, league sources told HoopsHype. Expect the Australian center to command offers north of the bi-annual exception in free agency.

item.title: Warriors expected to pursue Jock Landale
item.description: League sources say that the Warriors, though, are planning to register interest in various other free agents while waiting to see how James' highly anticipated free agency plays out. Sources say that Golden State is among the teams expected to pursue veteran big man Jock Landale in addition to scoring guards such as Anfernee Simons and Collin Sexton.

item.title: For those asking about Anfernee Simons in the wake of …
item.description: Barry Jackson: For those asking about Anfernee Simons in the wake of Bobby Marks saying he would be perfect fit at the minimum for Heat, feedback I've received is that he will enter free agency looking for more than that, understandably (averaged 14.9 ppg, 38.1 on 3s in career). There are always players who fall through cracks and take minimums (like Hardaway with Denver last year, Oubre with 76ers two years ago, etc. etc.). Heat will be poised to pounce if the right player falls through the cra

item.title: A memorial service was held Monday morning for Chicago …
item.description: A memorial service was held Monday morning for Chicago Bulls broadcaster and three-time NBA champion Stacey King. The service was held at the Advocate Center, a training facility close to the United Center.

item.title: 2026 First-round pick Bennett Stirtz: 'I thought I was going to be Division II my whole career'
item.description: During the pre-NBA Draft process, Bennett Stirtz worked out with the Chicago Bulls, Oklahoma City Thunder, Golden State Warriors, Memphis Grizzlies, Toronto Raptors and others. Now he’s in New York, getting ready for his name to be called at the 2026 NBA Draft. A few years ago, not even Stirtz thought this was possible. “Not at all,” Stirtz said. “I thought I was going to be Division II my whole career.”

item.title: Following the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to the Miami …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Following the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to the Miami Heat, there’s a growing sense within league circles that All-Star guard Norman Powell won’t return. The Chicago Bulls are among the teams expected to pursue Powell as free agency nears, as first reported in my story Sunday.

item.title: For those who ask 'why not dump Jovic to create more …
item.description: Barry Jackson: Powell can simply sign into Bulls cap space if he chooses. For those who ask 'why not dump Jovic to create more money' -- answer is it's a very hard contract to move and the Heat historically has been opposed to attaching first rounders to a negative asset simply to get off money. That has been a bridge too far for them over the years.

item.title: Players who have contract guarantee triggers today: …
item.description: Keith Smith: Players who have contract guarantee triggers today: 🏀Cam Christie - LAC: full at $2.3M 🏀Kris Dunn - LAC: full at $5.7M 🏀Kyle Filipowski - UTA: full at $3M 🏀Kam Jones - CHI: full at $2.2M 🏀Leonard Miller - CHI: full at $2.4M 🏀Svi Mykhailiuk - UTA: full at $3.8M

item.title: Bulls project having roughly $31M and the $9.4M room …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Bulls project having roughly $31M and the $9.4M room exception as free agency begins. With Leonard Miller set to return and Nic Claxton set to arrive, Bulls are at 12 contracts and two of their three 2-way contracts

item.title: Chicago Bulls will fully guarantee the $2.41 million …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Chicago Bulls will fully guarantee the $2.41 million salary of Leonard Miller for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype. Miller averaged 11.7 points on 55.5% shooting from the field with 5.8 rebounds in 27 games, including 12 starts, for Chicago last season.

item.title: Little over $1M guaranteed.
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Little over $1M guaranteed. Bryson Graham didn’t commit to Jones when asked about his future at news conference introducing Caleb Wilson and Dailyn Swain. Bulls still shy of salary floor as FA set to begin.

item.title: Bulls will waive Kam Jones
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Chicago Bulls plan to waive Kam Jones, league sources told @hoopshype . Jones had a deadline today that would’ve fully guaranteed his salary at $2.15 million. He was the 38th pick in the 2025 NBA Draft by the Indiana Pacers and was traded to Chicago on draft night.

item.title: Western Kentucky’s Grant Newell will join the Chicago …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Western Kentucky’s Grant Newell will join the Chicago Bulls for NBA Summer League, sources tell DraftExpress. The broad-framed, athletic 6’9”, 225 pound forward averaged 13 points and hit 58 threes for the Hilltoppers this season. Suiting up for his hometown team.

item.title: Zach Collins signs two-year extension with Bulls
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent center Zach Collins has agreed to a two-year, $17 million contract extension to stay with the Chicago Bulls, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: And the Bulls worked over the last few days to beat …
item.description: Shams Charania: Mark Bartelstein of @PrioritySports and the Bulls worked over the last few days to beat the 11:59 pm ET deadline tonight to complete the deal as an extension and keep Collins off the free agency market.

item.title: They should still maintain $32 million in cap space
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Bulls can give Zach Collins this contract with their $9.4 million room mid-level. They should still maintain $32 million in cap space.

item.title: Bulls at roughly $23M of cap space with room exception …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Bulls at roughly $23M of cap space with room exception of $9.4M at disposal and 13 players projected to be under contract

item.title: Deal is for two years, $17M with team option for …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Deal is for two years, $17M with team option for second season, per source

item.title: The structure of Powell’s deal gives Chicago …
item.description: After extending center Collins on Tuesday, the Bulls entered Day 2 of free agency with around $23 million in salary-cap space and the room exception of $9.4 million. With Norman Powell’s deal, the Bulls are essentially left to operate with that room exception. The structure of Powell’s deal gives Chicago flexibility to pivot after this season. It also gives it the chance to potentially capitalize on Powell’s value at the trade deadline.

item.title: Norman Powell, Bulls agree to a two-year, $45 million contract
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Chicago Bulls and Norman Powell have agreed to a two-year, $45 million deal. Powell is coming off his first All-Star season and is a career 40% 3-point shooter.

item.title: Lakers add veteran point guard Collin Sexton
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent Collin Sexton has agreed to a two-year, $19 million deal with the Los Angeles Lakers, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Sexton's new Lakers deal includes a player option, …
item.description: Shams Charania: Sexton's new Lakers deal includes a player option, sources said, after being finalized by CAA Sports' Austin Brown and Max Saidman.

item.title: OFFICIAL: We have exercised the team option on F …
item.description: Chicago Bulls: OFFICIAL: We have exercised the team option on F Leonard Miller. Welcome back, @LeonardMi11er

item.title: Bradley Beal, Anfernee Simons, Kentavious Caldwell-Pope on Miami's radar
item.description: A source said some of the bigger guards on Miami’s radar are 6-4 Bradley Beal, 6-3 Anfernee Simons and 6-5 Kentavious Caldwell Pope (who is expected to part ways with the Grizzlies). The 6-5 Zach LaVine could become an option if the Kings waive and stretch the final season (and $49 million) of his contract. And so could....

item.title: Anfernee Simons joins 76ers
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Free agent guard Anfernee Simons has agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, with a player option in second season, sources tell ESPN. Simons chose the 76ers over other suitors believing his fit is perfect with the revamped 76ers roster.

item.title: Tobias Harris says "urgency to win a championship" is why he joined Spurs
item.description: As for what factored ultimately in his decision to choose the Spurs, Harris told HoopsHype via text message, “The urgency to win a championship. It felt like both our goals aligned.”

item.title: That second-year team option makes Norman Powell …
item.description: KC Johnson: "Let's start with the Bulls agreeing to terms with Norman Powell on a two-year, $45 million deal. By now, if you're watching this, you know the second year is a team option. This to me is a fantastic deal on so many levels. Let's start with the financial components. That second-year team option makes Norman Powell immediately tradeable (...) If the Bulls want to pivot off Norman Powell at next February's trade deadline, and some team wants to add a very, very good player in Norman Po

item.title: Tiago Splitter will coach the first couple Summer League games for Bulls
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Bulls Summer League roster is here. Tiago Splitter will coach the first couple games, then a couple remaining assistants from Billy Donovan’s staff in Henry Domercant, Austin Dufault and Martin Rancik.

item.title: Bulls make official signings of Tobe Awaka and Jaylin …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Bulls make official signings of Tobe Awaka and Jaylin Sellers to two-way contracts

item.title: No. 4 draft pick Caleb Wilson: Basketball wasn't my favorite sport at first, it was baseball
item.description: Before Caleb Wilson became a basketball superstar, his heart actually belonged to another sport — baseball. Speaking with PEOPLE exclusively ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft in New York City on June 23, the athlete, 19, said of basketball, "It actually wasn't my favorite sport at first. It was baseball." "My dad inspired me to do it," he continued, referring to his father, Jerry Wilson. "I played a lot of sports when I was younger, but basketball is what I stuck with."

item.title: Sharing a motto instilled in him by his father, Wilson …
item.description: Sharing a motto instilled in him by his father, Wilson said: “If you’re sleeping in, somebody’s working.” “It's all worth it in the end, to have the lifestyle we have, and to have the ability to do the things and see the things were able to see," he further explained.

item.title: Tiago Splitter said he plans to coach two games at NBA …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Tiago Splitter said he plans to coach two games at NBA Summer League as head coach. “I want to set the tone, especially having guys like Caleb (Wilson) and Dailyn (Swain). They’re going to be important for this organization.”

item.title: Caleb Wilson on his early impressions of Tiago …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Caleb Wilson on his early impressions of Tiago Splitter “First, he has a big accent. He’s really detailed. And he’s pretty serious. You have coaches that are joking around and stuff like that, Tiago’s pretty serious. He knows what he wants, he wants it now.”

item.title: Noa Essengue said he feels “amazing” but said he …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Noa Essengue said he feels “amazing” but said he doesn’t yet know if the plan is for him to play all five games at NBA Summer League

item.title: Tiago Splitter, asked what position Noa Essengue might …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Tiago Splitter, asked what position Noa Essengue might spend most of his time with the Summer Bulls, says “he could play 3, 4 or 5, easily.”

item.title: Reporter: “Thoughts on Sunday’s World Cup action?” …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Reporter: “Thoughts on Sunday’s World Cup action?” Tiago Splitter, smiling: “Terrible.”

item.title: Noa Essengue said with his new shoulder he has to …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Noa Essengue said with his new shoulder he has to “learn how to play again.” Says he’s at about 98% and is still working through some range-of-motion stuff. Said he hasn’t talked to the staff about how many games he’ll play in Vegas.

item.title: "In the old days, you knew [Larry Bird's] Celtics were …
item.description: It's a dangerous path to specifically go after the Knicks, but it doesn't feel as if they're going anywhere. That is, of course, a similar thought to the way the league felt about the 2025 Thunder, the 2024 Celtics and the 2023 Denver Nuggets. "In the old days, you knew [Larry Bird's] Celtics were at the top and you had to chase them. Then it was Isiah [Thomas] and Detroit. Then Michael [Jordan] and the Bulls," the executive said. "It's not like that anymore. You have to build a sustainable winn

item.title: Michael Jordan to headline SBC Summit 2026 in Lisbon
item.description: The SBC Summit 2026, one of the largest global events dedicated to the sports, betting and entertainment industry, taking place in Lisbon between September 29th and October 1st, promises a strong lineup this year. The highlight of the first day will be the keynote address entitled "Leadership in Sports Business ," in which Michael Jordan will take center stage. The legendary number 23 of the Chicago Bulls will share his perspectives and experiences not only as a sports icon, but also as a succes

item.title: Tiago Splitter said he expects Dailyn Swain to be on …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Tiago Splitter said he expects Dailyn Swain to be on the ball and run plenty of offense in Vegas. Between him and Swain, the term “big guard” was used a lot today. Swain sounds like he really wants to hone in on being a secondary ball handler to relieve pressure from PGs in NBA.

item.title: Tiago Splitter said he’s “not done yet” with staff …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: As we’ve relayed on @CHSN__, Tiago Splitter said he’s “not done yet” with staff hirings. As for holdovers like Wes Unseld Jr. and player development staffers, Splitter said”it’s important to keep on guys who know the players well. A mix of guys who were here and new guys is good”

item.title: Guerschon Yabusele signs with Panathinaikos
item.description: Michael Scotto: Free agent forward Guerschon Yabusele has agreed to a three-year deal with Panathinaikos, league sources told @hoopshype. Yabusele will be one of the top three highest-paid European players. He's averaged 8.3 points over the past two NBA seasons with the 76ers, Bulls and Knicks.

item.title: Caleb Wilson: I want to be better than Michael Jordan
item.description: When asked about Jordan after being drafted by the Bulls, Caleb Wilson said: “I want to be better than Mike. Honestly, I know it’s a lot to [reach that goal]. But I’m writing my own story.”

item.title: “Everybody has the right to their own opinion, so I …
item.description: Caleb Wilson is known for having a long memory for slights against him dating back to his high school days, and falling to fourth only fueled the motivated rookie with superstar expectations. “Everybody has the right to their own opinion, so I don’t really care,” Wilson told Andscape. “I’ll have the opportunity to play against everybody.”

item.title: “I expect to [win] Rookie of the Year, honestly,” …
item.description: A 6-foot-9, 210-pound forward, Wilson shot 57.8% from the field and had thrown down a nation-leading 67 dunks when his season ended. He also has a wingspan just more than 7 feet, a 9-foot standing reach and a 39½-inch running vertical. “I expect to [win] Rookie of the Year, honestly,” Wilson said at his Bulls introductory news conference. “I’m going to work hard. I’m going to do what it takes. I feel like this team is really good for me for how I envision to play. And I know I’m a hard worker. S

item.title: “Honestly, it was a rough point,” he said. “But I just …
item.description: Wilson said being sidelined by injury was “the hardest time of my life.” “Honestly, it was a rough point,” he said. “But I just healed. I healed way faster than I was supposed to, and I just got back to work, honestly. But it just made me grateful for the opportunity to play, and then just kind of added more fuel to my fire now that I’m back playing and have the opportunity to play the summer league. … I’m kind of coming with the same kind of energy and vengeance I have to.”

item.title: The Bulls have announced that they acquired Nic …
item.description: Brett Siegel: The Bulls have announced that they acquired Nic Claxton from the Nets, which means the four-team deal involving Chicago, Brooklyn, Charlotte, and Minnesota that also involves Julius Randle and LaMelo Ball is now COMPLETE.

item.title: Naz Reid, Mouhamadou Gueye, MIN 2033 1st …
item.description: Brett Siegel: Trade details: Hornets: Naz Reid, Mouhamadou Gueye, MIN 2033 1st (unprotected), MIN 2028, 2029, 2030 1st SWAPS, MIN 2029, 2032, 2033 2nds Nets: Julius Randle, Joshua Jefferson (Pick No. 28) Timberwolves: LaMelo Ball, Josh Green, Isaiah Evans (Pick No. 33) Bulls: Nic Claxton

item.title: We have signed All-Star G Norman Powell
item.description: Chicago Bulls: OFFICIAL: We have signed All-Star G Norman Powell. Welcome to Chicago, Norman! 🫡

item.title: Caleb Wilson explodes for 35 points in Summer League debut
item.description: Underdog NBA: Caleb Wilson in Summer League debut: 35 PTS, 5 REB, 3 BLK, 2 STL, 6 TO, 12-21 FG, 7-11 3PT

item.title: Before this, the first thing he cited when asked how …
item.description: KC Johnson: Told that his 35 points set an unofficial NBA Summer League record for most points in a debut, Caleb Wilson said “we lost.” Before this, the first thing he cited when asked how he played was too many TOs and missed boxouts.

item.title: "It's been 5 months to the day since the last time I …
item.description: Underdog NBA: Caleb Wilson said he cried before his Summer League debut: "It's been 5 months to the day since the last time I played … I felt terrible because my team lost in the tournament, and my coach got fired. It just was a lot for me."

item.title: "It's been 5 months to the day since the last time I …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Caleb Wilson reveals he cried before his Summer League debut tonight "It's been 5 months to the day since the last time I played… I felt terrible because my team lost in the tournament, and my coach got fired. It just was a lot for me." (Via @JoelXLorenzi)

item.title: “I cried before I played today,” the Chicago Bulls …
item.description: “I cried before I played today,” the Chicago Bulls rookie said after tallying 35 points, five boards and three blocks in his Las Vegas Summer League debut, a 97-96 loss to the Memphis Grizzlies. “It’s been five months to the day since the last time I played, and I’ve just been really emotional because I haven’t been able to play. I felt terrible because my team lost in the tournament and my coach got fired. It was a lot for me at that point. “So coming out today, it just felt like I’ve been wait

item.title: Dailyn Swain on Tiago Splitter: “He’s always intense, …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Dailyn Swain on Tiago Splitter: “He’s always intense, passionate about the game. He was a player, so he did all this stuff before. He was always motivating, never like ‘C’mon guys.’ You can see how much he loves the game, so it was easy to give your all for a coach like that.”

item.title: “I go in every situation wanting to be where I’m at …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Norman Powell on the flexible nature of his deal: “I go in every situation wanting to be where I’m at and help win. Looking at the business of it, I’ve been around so long that it crossed my mind. But I’m focused on being in the moment.” Said he wanted to return to Miami but is understanding of the apron’s effect on teams and wants to be where he’s valued.

item.title: “Everybody’s super young. I think Im the only …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Norman Powell says he wants to help the Bulls young guys find their paths. “Everybody’s super young. I think Im the only 30-year-old on the team. So just help them, guide them. Focus on the details and how to improve and play winning basketball.”

item.title: “I've never needed 25 shots to get my stuff off. I …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Norman Powell: “I think this is an opportunity for me that I've always wanted. I wanted to be a vocal leader. I wanted to lead by example. “I've never needed 25 shots to get my stuff off. I play within the flow of the game. I try to play the game the right way, and I know these guys have a great opportunity.”

item.title: will wear No. 24 for the #Bulls.
item.description: Etienne Catalan: Norman Powell ( @npowell2404 ) will wear No. 24 for the #Bulls. Number last worn by Noa Essengue in 2026. #NBA

item.title: Bulls rookie Caleb Wilson taking at least 2,000 shots every day: 'You get more confidence putting in the work'
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Caleb Wilson says he’s taking 2k-2,500 shots everyday. Says he’s not changing anything mechanically. “You get more confidence putting in the work.” At UNC: “I didn’t really have the time. Had to go to class. That’s what’s good about the NBA, it’s just your job.”

item.title: Former Bulls GM Arturas Karnisovas joins NBA Europe project
item.description: The NBA hired former Lithuanian star and Chicago Bulls vice president Artūras Karnišovas as its consultant for building NBA Europe, according to sources with direct knowledge of the hiring who were granted anonymity to discuss a personnel matter that had not been formally announced. Karnišovas, 55, will advise NBA commissioner Adam Silver and his deputies on governance and game rules for NBA Europe, how to identify and recruit talent, how to create pathways for players into the new league and

item.title: Norman Powell: My lowest point was my third year in the league in Toronto
item.description: Norman Powell: My lowest point was my third year in the league in Toronto. I just came off of a great playoff run in my second year in the league helping them close out a win series and I signed my contract extension and I worked extremely hard. Looking back I kind of overworked myself and not being smart about how I took care of my body in the summer and I crashed and burned during the season. I had a really bad season and I signed my contract before and just everybody was talking about like I

item.title: How do you feel you can help this team both on and off …
item.description: How do you feel you can help this team both on and off the court? Norman Powell: "I just think my overall experience and knowledge of the game on the court, helping them find their own path. Everybody is super young. I think I'm the only 30-year-old on the team, 30 plus. So, just helping guide them, giving them as much knowledge that I've gained and received over the years through vets that I've had, and then just helping them focus on the details of their game and how to improve and how we can

item.title: Utah Jazz guard Trey Alexander was taken from the …
item.description: Utah Jazz guard Trey Alexander was taken from the court on a stretcher Monday night after appearing to injure his side on a drive to the basket in an NBA summer league game against the Chicago Bulls. Alexander, who signed a two-way deal with Utah last week, made contact with the Bulls' Caleb Wilson while driving toward the basket and, after tossing the ball toward the hoop, went behind the basket clutching his side or abdomen. He then dropped to the ground, seemingly in great pain. The 23-year-o
